sections.paragraph:
for מאֹסֵר (from the root אָסַר), only in plural ־ִים and ֹות-m. bonds; specially used of the bonds of a yoke, often metaphorically, Psa 2:3 107:14 116:16 Isa 28:22 52:2 Jer 5:5 27:2 .

(2) pr.n. מֹוסֵר and ֹות [Mosera, Moseroth], pr.n. of a station of the Israelites in the desert, Num 33:30 Deu 10:6.
